skill.name: thinking-partner
skill.description: 思考拍档 - 陪你从混沌中理清局面，锁定核心问题，拆解卡点，共创解法，落地行动

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the single Markdown skill file and were dismissed as false positives. The detected backticks are a fenced workflow diagram, the flagged description line contains no cryptographic algorithm, and the file is readable Markdown rather than obfuscated content.

low_findings.title: False positive: fenced workflow diagram
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but lines 33-35 are a Markdown fenced block showing the five-stage workflow. No executable command or interpreter invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a Markdown code fence containing Chinese workflow labels and arrows. There is no shell syntax, Ruby interpolation, or user-controlled command execution.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 3 as weak cryptography, but this line is the YAML description for the skill. It does not name or use any cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text describing a thinking-partner workflow. No hashing, encryption, key handling, or algorithm selection appears there.

low_findings.title: False positive: high entropy content
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged high file entropy at line 1, but the file is readable Markdown with YAML front matter and Chinese prose. No binary blob, encrypted payload, or encoded content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is human-readable instructions and contains no hidden payload indicators. The entropy signal is likely caused by multilingual UTF-8 text.

content.user_title: Clarify Problems With a Thinking Partner
content.value_statement: Complex situations often feel like many problems at once. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through a staged conversation that identifies the core issue, root cause, solution direction, and action plan.
actual_capabilities: Runs a five-stage dialogue from information gathering to action planning.
Asks focused questions before offering analysis or advice.
Helps separate core problems from secondary issues and noise.
Challenges new information by weighing whether it changes the main judgment.
Turns an agreed solution direction into practical next actions.
limitations: Does not make decisions for the user or replace expert advice.
Requires user confirmation before moving between major stages.
Works best when the user provides real context and constraints.
Does not include tools, scripts, data access, or automated execution.

use_cases.title: Clarify a Stuck Personal Decision
use_cases.description: Use the staged questions to separate emotions, constraints, and the real decision point.

use_cases.title: Diagnose a Work Problem
use_cases.description: Identify the main blocker behind scattered workplace issues before choosing an action.

use_cases.title: Shape a Practical Plan
use_cases.description: Convert an agreed direction into a simple plan that includes what to do and what to stop doing.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Clarity Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as my thinking partner. Start with information gathering only. Ask me two or three focused questions about the situation before analyzing anything.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Core Problem
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me identify the core problem in this situation. First ask what goal, constraints, attempts, and concerns matter most. Then explain which issue seems decisive.

prompt_templates.title: Challenge My Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the thinking-partner workflow to test my current view. Separate signal from noise, tell me what changes your judgment, and ask for confirmation before moving on.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Action Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through all five stages. After we agree on the root cause and solution direction, turn the result into a practical plan with priorities and things to stop doing.

output_examples.input: I feel stuck because my project has too many problems at once.
output_examples.output: The assistant first asks about your role, goal, recent attempts, and constraints. It does not analyze until you confirm the key information is complete.

output_examples.input: I think my issue is lack of discipline, but I am not sure.
output_examples.output: The assistant tests whether discipline is the core issue or a symptom. It asks for evidence from past successes and failures before naming the root cause.

output_examples.input: We agreed on the solution direction. Help me make it real.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks about available time and energy, then creates a short action plan with priorities and explicit non-actions.

best_practices: Give concrete context about goals, constraints, history, and risks.
Answer stage-gate questions clearly before asking for the next phase.
Push back when the assistant's core-problem hypothesis misses important evidence.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a quick answer when the situation needs diagnosis.
Do not skip information gathering if the facts are still unclear.
Do not use this skill for medical, legal, financial, or safety-critical decisions without expert review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It turns the assistant into a structured thinking partner for complex personal or work problems.

faq.question: Does it give direct advice immediately?
faq.answer: No. It first gathers context, then confirms the core problem before discussing solutions.

faq.question: Can it help with business decisions?
faq.answer: Yes, it can structure the discussion, clarify tradeoffs, and create an action plan.

faq.question: Does it run code or access files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is only a Markdown conversation framework and includes no executable tools.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I avoid using it?
faq.answer: Avoid relying on it as the only source for regulated, clinical, legal, or high-risk decisions.
